top_categories.workout_context: 75 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
GFNY Uppsala's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 75 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Uppsala's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At GFNY Uppsala, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Uppsala. Train it until it feels boring.
5,906ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At GFNY Uppsala, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for GFNY Uppsala. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
5,906ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 75 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.heat: August in Uppsala, Sweden can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before GFNY Uppsala. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for GFNY Uppsala’s 75 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at GFNY Uppsala requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 75 miles of mixed terrain in Uppsala, Sweden.
